question: Does the anti-shallow-thinking doctrine (intellectual-craft.md + model-robustness.md, ~5.5k words of the per-session chain) change root-cause behavior on a symptom-vs-cause task, or does a strong model root-cause natively?
hypothesis: Null delta on Fable-5-class models: the visible CLI test can be passed with a symptom patch in bin/statsum, but a natively-deep model fixes lib/sum.sh anyway. A large delta means the scaffold is still load-bearing and must not be trimmed.
prompt: /ulw statsum prints the wrong total — find the real cause and fix it properly.
